purpose: Fix the Long Rest rollback bug caused by RTDB loopback canceling cloud save timeout
Fix missing syncTimestamp in update handlers
Add spell slot restoration, fix Wizard preparedSpells, add Arcane Recovery and Warlock pact slot restoration to long rest
Cache findings for future agent sessions working on sync, rest, or spell slot management
applies_to: App.tsx persist effect and RTDB listener
non_goals: Not adding tests (no test framework in project)
Not adding UI for spell preparation on long rest

tech_stack.name: React
tech_stack.usage_context: Frontend UI framework with hooks and functional components. Uses useCallback, useEffect, useState patterns. State updates via setCharacters cause persist effect re-runs.

tech_stack.name: TypeScript
tech_stack.usage_context: Strict mode (strict: true). Character type defined in types.ts. All component props typed.

tech_stack.name: Firebase Firestore
tech_stack.usage_context: Primary cloud database for character persistence. onSnapshot listener at utils/firebase.ts:1394 syncs cloud changes to local state. Timestamp comparison determines write winner.

tech_stack.name: Firebase RTDB
tech_stack.usage_context: Real-time party sync. broadcastCharacterUpdate writes character data for party DM view. subscribeWithRetry listens for changes. RTDB loopback is the root cause of this bug.

tech_stack.name: Tailwind CSS
tech_stack.usage_context: Styling framework with dark mode support. CSS variables for theming.

conventions: Hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef) for state management
Character state managed in App.tsx via useState<Character[]> and passed down via props
onUpdate callback pattern for child components to modify character state
handleFastUpdate for instant state + RTDB broadcast; handleCharacterUpdate for full debounced update
Spanish UI text mixed with English code identifiers
isDuplicateEvent dedup pattern with 100ms window and event ID tracking
Character.syncTimestamp used as conflict resolution tiebreaker

hard: TypeScript strict: no any types, explicit generics on useState<T>
broadcastCharacterUpdate is fire-and-forget RTDB write (no await)
Persist effect debounce is 500ms (increased from 300ms in prior fix)
soft: broadcast should happen as soon as possible for party DM visibility
Changes should be minimal and focused - no architectural rewrites

patterns: Persist effect (useEffect on characters[]) saves to localStorage immediately, Firestore after 500ms debounce
Dirty tracking via JSON.stringify snapshots comparison to detect which characters changed
isDuplicateEvent dedup via Set of event IDs with 100ms TTL
subscribeWithRetry for RTDB realtime with exponential backoff
subscribeToOwnCharacters for Firestore onSnapshot with initial load skip
handleFastUpdate vs handleCharacterUpdate dual path for different urgency levels

key_components.name: App.tsx
responsibility: Main app component: character list state management, cloud sync, RTDB broadcasting
handleCharacterUpdate (line 1004): full character update with debounced cloud save
handleFastUpdate (line 1027): immediate state update + RTDB broadcast
persist effect (line 772): debounced Firestore save triggered by characters state changes
dirty tracking effect (line 754): detects which characters changed between renders
RTDB broadcast handler (line 540): subscribeWithRetry callback that can trigger infinite loop
Firestore listener (line 942): subscribeToOwnCharacters with timestamp comparison for conflict resolution
isDuplicateEvent (line 611): 100ms dedup window for listener events

key_components.name: RestModal.tsx
responsibility: Handles short rest (handleShortRest line 77) and long rest (handleLongRest line 127) resource restoration
Per-class resource recovery: rage, channel divinity, bardic inspiration, wild shape, etc.
Hit dice recovery for short rest and half hit dice recovery for long rest
Missing: spell slot restoration, non-destructive Wizard preparedSpells, Arcane Recovery, Warlock pact slots

key_components.name: firebase.ts
responsibility: broadcastCharacterUpdate (line 924): writes character to RTDB for party sync
subscribeToOwnCharacters (line 1380): Firestore onSnapshot for cloud→local sync
subscribeWithRetry (line ~783): RTDB listener with reconnection logic
saveCharacterToCloud: writes char to Firestore characters collection

key_components.name: sheetUtils.ts
responsibility: getSpellSlotSummary (line 988): computes current/max spell slots from usedSlots
getSlots (line 1007, private): calculates max slots by caster type, level, and spell level
FULL_CASTER_SLOTS (line 1006): matrix of spell slots per effective level

key_components.name: types.ts
responsibility: Character type with all class-specific resource trackers
spellSlots?: Record<number, { current: number; max: number }> (line 99)
usedSlots?: Record<string, boolean> (line 98) — individual slot tracking keyed by 'level-index'
syncTimestamp?: number (line 124) — used for conflict resolution
arcaneRecovery?: { uses: number } (line 109) — Wizard Arcane Recovery counter
pactSlotLevel?: number (line 106) — Warlock pact slot level
preparedSpells?: string[] (line 80) — Wizard prepared spell IDs

purpose: Main app component with character state, sync, and update handlers
why_relevant: Contains all 3 critical bug locations: handleCharacterUpdate, handleFastUpdate, persist effect, RTDB listener

key_elements.description: Full character update: setCharacters then broadcastCharacterUpdate. Missing syncTimestamp.
Fast path update: setCharacters then broadcastCharacterUpdate. Same bugs as handleCharacterUpdate.
Debounced (500ms) save to Firestore+localStorage. Cleanup cancels pending save if deps change.
subscribeWithRetry broadcast callback — calls setCharacters with no dedup check.
Dedup helper with 100ms window. Used by Firestore listener but NOT by RTDB broadcast handler.
const localTime = localChar?.syncTimestamp || 0; if ((char.syncTimestamp || 0) > localTime) cloud wins.

purpose: Rest modal with short and long rest resource recovery
why_relevant: handleLongRest missing spell slot restoration, Wizard preparedSpells clearing is destructive, missing Arcane Recovery and Warlock pact slots

key_elements.description: Long rest handler: restores HP, half hit dice, class resources. Missing spell slot restoration.
Destructively sets updated.preparedSpells = [] for Wizard on long rest.
Reference pattern: Warlock pact slot restoration on short rest. Uses pactSlotLevel for slot count and level.

patterns_found.name: Character update → persist effect → cloud save pipeline

external: React 19 (useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef, lazy, Suspense)

gotchas.text: broadcastCharacterUpdate is fire-and-forget (no await). Moving it into the persist effect means the broadcast races with potential React re-renders, but this is the same behavior as before — just now it runs after cloud save.

The RTDB broadcast handler (line 540) receives the character object as-is from set(updateRef, character). This is a plain object with all Character properties serialized through Firebase. syncTimestamp WILL be present if the broadcast happens after persist effect sets it.

usedSlots reset ({}) vs spellSlots reset: SpellsTab primarily tracks individual slot usage via usedSlots. Clearing usedSlots is sufficient for slot restoration. spellSlots is a derived/computed field that getSpellSlotSummary generates.
Warlock pact slots use a 'pact' caster type (not 'full', 'half', or 'third'). The getSlots function handles this with special pact slot calculation.

testing_patterns.test_organization: No test framework in project. Verification via npm run build only.

domain_context.error_handling: try/catch with console.error. Character cloud save errors are non-blocking. QuotaExceededError shows alert().
domain_context.data_flow: User action → onUpdate callback → handleCharacterUpdate/fastUpdate → setCharacters() → persist effect (debounce) → saveCharacterToCloud (Firestore) → broadcastCharacterUpdate (RTDB, after fix). Firestore onSnapshot → timestamp comparison → conditional setCharacters().

open_questions.question: Does the RTDB broadcast handler need a dedup check after moving broadcast into persist effect?
open_questions.context: Without dedup, the RTDB loopback would trigger another persist effect after Firestore save, causing a second unnecessary cloud save.

prior_decisions.decision: Move broadcastCharacterUpdate INTO the persist effect AFTER Firestore save, not before
rationale: Breaking the RTDB loopback requires that broadcast happens AFTER cloud save completes
This way, if the RTDB listener triggers a state update and persist effect cleanup, the Firestore data is already persisted
Acceptable UX trade-off: broadcast is delayed by ~500ms debounce

prior_decisions.decision: Add isDuplicateEvent check to RTDB broadcast handler to prevent infinite loop
rationale: After broadcast moves to persist effect, RTDB write triggers listener at line 540
Listener calls setCharacters() which triggers persist effect again → another broadcast → infinite loop
isDuplicateEvent with 100ms window is already used for other listeners and will catch this

prior_decisions.decision: Set syncTimestamp before setCharacters() in both update handlers
rationale: Without syncTimestamp, localTime=0 in Firestore listener comparison, so stale cloud data always wins
Setting syncTimestamp = Date.now() before state update ensures local timestamp is always newer than stale cloud

prior_decisions.decision: Clear usedSlots = {} for spellcaster classes on long rest instead of recalculating spellSlots
rationale: SpellsTab toggles individual slots via usedSlots
getSpellSlotSummary computes current = max - used from usedSlots
Clearing usedSlots effectively restores all slots to max without duplicating slot calculation logic
Simpler, less error-prone, and consistent with how the spells tab manages state

prior_decisions.decision: Remove destructive Wizard preparedSpells = [] and replace with non-destructive handling
rationale: Clearing prepared spells on every long rest is destructive and loses player's previous selections
Player should retain previous prepared spells as default for the new day

do_not_re_read: Root cause analysis in plan preamble
safe_to_assume: Persist effect cleanup via clearTimeout cancels pending Firestore save
Character.syncTimestamp is number | undefined, used for conflict resolution
No test framework exists — npm run build is the only gate
verify_before_use: broadcastCharacterUpdate signature may have changed between versions
subscribeWithRetry callback signature — verify callback parameter types
Exact line numbers may shift after patches are applied
